what_it_does: CSS stylesheet that styles a full-page ProjectsScreen project viewer, including the page wrapper, header, filter toolbar, search/sort controls, a responsive grid of project cards with progress phase bars, an add-project tile, an empty state, and a mobile breakpoint.
observations: Uses a warm-paper/gold-ink color system via CSS custom properties like --accent-gold, --accent-bronze, --ink, and --font-display
Project cards (.pcard) use hover lift, image zoom, and a slide-in arrow icon
Filter chips (.projects__filter) have active states and small count badges (.projects__filter-n)
Includes a dashed add tile (.projects__add) and a centered empty state (.projects__empty)
Progress phases shown with a gradient fill bar inside a track
Search input is styled inside a focus-within wrapper; sort uses a native select
Media query at 720px stacks the header and reduces padding/title size
Does not define the custom properties it consumes; it relies on external token definitions
standouts: Cohesive warm gold/bronze visual identity applied consistently across cards, buttons, and progress bars
Card hover micro-interactions combining translateY, box-shadow, image scale, and arrow reveal
Filter chips with active state and count badges
conventions: BEM-like class naming: block--element modifiers such as .projects__head and .pcard__body
Component-scoped stylesheet with a single top-level block (.projects)
CSS custom properties used for theming, spacing, radii, and typography
Responsive adjustment via a single max-width media query
Interactive elements styled as buttons but no explicit button/appearance reset
Transitions declared with the broad all property in several places
concerns: .projects__new, .projects__filter, and .projects__add are styled as clickable controls but no explicit button/anchor reset or focus-visible styles are provided
.projects__search input has a fixed 150px width which may truncate on small screens
.projects__sort select relies on default appearance without an explicit appearance reset
Several transitions use all, which can animate unintended properties and impact performance
.pcard has border:0 and font-family:inherit, hinting it may be a button, but no focus or disabled states are defined
Color and font tokens are consumed but not defined in this file, creating an external dependency
.projects__new span b/small and .pcard__meta markup assumptions are not documented
notes: A self-contained, warm-gold themed stylesheet for a project browsing screen with cards, filters, and responsive tweaks, but it assumes externally defined design tokens and specific HTML structure.
questions: Where are the CSS custom properties such as --accent-gold, --ink, --font-display, and --ease-out defined?
Is .pcard intended to be a <button>, an <a>, or a generic clickable container?
How are the filter count values in .projects__filter-n populated and updated?
What is the expected HTML structure inside .projects__new (span > b + small) and .pcard__meta?
Is ProjectsScreen rendered inside a specific Studio shell canvas class that provides additional layout context?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it styles a dedicated full-page project viewer with a warm-paper/gold-ink theme.
apparent_themes: Warm editorial aesthetic with premium gold/bronze accents
Card-based browsing with tactile hover feedback
Clear typographic hierarchy and compact UI controls
intention_signals: To create an inviting, studio-like project dashboard that feels crafted and premium, using warm metallic accents and subtle motion to guide attention without overwhelming the user.
novelty: A distinct warm-paper/gold-ink visual identity combined with project-specific phase progress bars integrated directly into each card.
contribution: Provides a complete, self-contained stylesheet for a project browsing screen, including header, filter toolbar, search/sort controls, responsive card grid, phase progress indicators, add tile, empty state, reduced-motion support, and a cohesive warm gold accent design language that a fused system could reuse.
summary_for_embedding: CSS stylesheet for a full-page ProjectsScreen project viewer with a warm gold/bronze theme, defining header, filter toolbar, search/sort controls, responsive project card grid, phase progress bars, add tile, and empty state.
intention_for_embedding: To implement a visually cohesive, accessible project browsing dashboard within a studio shell using warm paper/gold ink styling and subtle hover animations.
